content: Just as English language learners use deliberate lexical and structural reflection to reinforce long-term learning of new grammatical and conceptual patterns, large language models can leverage iterative human-style reflexion practice (rooted in the educational definition of guided reflection) to better align their outputs with consistent, contextually correct reasoning patterns, rather than relying solely on self-comparison of inconsistent solutions. This frames reflexion not as a niche AI technique, but as a domain-general learning reinforcement mechanism that works for both human and artificial learners.
